questions.question: A single narrative detailing one of Buddha's former existences is known by which of the following names?
questions.solution:
The Jatakas are a voluminous body of literature native to India concerning the previous births of Gautama Buddha in both human and animal form. The future Buddha may appear as a king, an outcast, a god, or an animal, but, in whatever form, he exhibits some virtue that the narrative inculcates.

  • Sutra: Refers to the sermons and discourses of the Buddha.
  • Tripitaka: The 'Three Baskets' (Vinaya, Sutta, and Abhidhamma) which constitute the traditional term for Buddhist scriptures.
  • Vinaya: The code of discipline for Buddhist monks and nuns.

questions.question: The Bhimbetka rock shelters, known for prehistoric cave paintings, are located in which of the following states of India?
questions.solution: The Bhimbetka rock shelters are an archaeological site in central India that spans the prehistoric Paleolithic and Mesolithic periods, as well as the historic period. They are located in the Raisen District in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh and are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: In the Pallava administration, which was the smallest administrative unit directly managed by local self-governing assemblies?
questions.solution:
In the Pallava administrative system, the hierarchy was designed to manage vast territories efficiently. The Ur was the smallest unit, representing a village assembly or the village itself, directly managed by the local residents. Other units included:

  • Mandala: The largest unit, similar to a province.
  • Kottam: A division within the Mandala.
  • Nadu: A district or a collection of several villages.

questions.question: The Parthians originated from which of the following countries?
questions.solution: The Parthians (often referred to as the Pahlavas in Indian texts) originally belonged to Iran. They moved into North-Western India and established a kingdom, the most famous ruler being Gondophernes.

questions.question: The first systematisation of the Jain canonical literature took place at ____.
questions.solution: The First Jain Council was held at Pataliputra (modern-day Patna) in the 4th century BC. It was presided over by Sthulabhadra and resulted in the compilation of the 12 Angas, marking the first major effort to systematize Jain canonical literature.

questions.question: The Lion Capital located at Sarnath, which is adopted as the national emblem of the Republic of India, was erected by which of the following Mauryan kings?
questions.solution: The Lion Capital of Sarnath was built by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BC to mark the spot where the Buddha first preached his Dhamma. It features four Asiatic lions standing back to back on an elaborate abacus.

questions.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT about the Harappans?
questions.option_1: They exported ornaments, timber, some objects of daily use.
questions.option_2: The most favoured ornaments were beads of carnelian.
questions.option_3: The Harappans were engaged in active trade.
questions.option_4: The Harappans used only gold to make tools, pots and pans.
questions.solution:
The Harappans lived in the Bronze Age. While they were technologically advanced, the statement that they used only gold is incorrect:

  • They primarily used copper and bronze to make tools, weapons, and vessels.
  • Gold and silver were used mainly for making jewelry and ornaments.
  • They were active traders, exporting beads (carnelian), ivory, and timber to regions like Mesopotamia.

questions.question: During the Satavahanas kingdom, what did the terms ‘ahara’ or ‘rashtra’ refer to?
questions.solution: In the Satavahana administration, the kingdom was divided into subdivisions for effective governance. These subdivisions or districts were referred to as ahara or rashtra, and they were often headed by officials known as Amatyas or Mahamatras.

questions.question: Who were the Voharikas in the judicial structure of the Magadha period?
questions.solution: During the Magadha period and early Buddhist era, the judicial system became more structured. The Voharikas were the Lawyer-Judges or high-ranking judicial officers responsible for interpreting laws and delivering justice at the royal courts.

questions.question: The Eran stone inscription states that Samudragupta was chosen as king by his father because of his ______.
questions.solution: The Eran stone inscription, found in Madhya Pradesh, is a significant source for Gupta history. It praises Samudragupta, noting that his father, Chandragupta I, selected him as the successor because of his exceptional devotion, righteous conduct, and valour in battle, bypassing other contenders.

questions.question: The Shaka dynasty in India was founded by _____.
questions.solution: The Shaka (Indo-Scythian) dynasty in India was established by Maues (also known as Moga) in the 1st century BC. He extended Shaka power over Gandhara and established his capital at Sirkap (Taxila).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a principle of Jainism?
questions.option_1: Rejection of the doctrine of Karma
questions.option_2: Practice of non-injury to living beings
questions.option_3: The whole world, including stone and water, has life.
questions.option_4: Monastic life is the only way to salvation.
questions.solution: Jainism is centered on the concept of spiritual liberation through rigorous asceticism and non-violence. The rejection of the doctrine of Karma is NOT a principle of Jainism; in fact, Jains believe that Karma is a physical substance that attaches to the soul and must be shed to achieve Kaivalya (salvation).

questions.question: Which of the following Vedas mentions the names of some learned ladies like Viswavara, Apala and Ghosa who composed mantras and attained the rank of Rishis?
questions.solution: The Rigveda, the oldest of the four Vedas, reflects a society where some women held high spiritual status. It mentions female seers known as Brahmavadinis, such as Viswavara, Apala, and Ghosa, who composed Vedic hymns and were regarded as Rishis.

questions.question: What feature of Indus Valley town planning indicates advanced civic amenities and engineering skills of the civilisation?
questions.solution: The Indus Valley Civilisation is renowned for its urban planning. The most striking feature was the sophisticated drainage system. Every house was connected to street drains, which were covered with stone slabs or bricks and equipped with manholes for cleaning—a level of hygiene unmatched by other contemporary civilisations.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Buddhist text containing stories related to the previous births of the Buddha?
questions.solution: The Jatakas are specific Buddhist texts that contain approximately 550 stories regarding the previous lives of the Buddha. These stories often use animals or humans to teach moral and ethical lessons relevant to Buddhist philosophy.

questions.question: The Harappans were first in Eurasia to grow ___.
questions.solution: The Harappan people were the earliest known producers of cotton in the world. Because it was first grown in the Indus region, the Greeks referred to it as Sindon (derived from Sindh).

questions.question: The title Devaputra, used by the Kushana rulers, implies which of the following?
questions.option_4: Divine origin or claim to divinity
questions.solution: The Kushana rulers, most notably Kanishka, adopted the title Devaputra, which translates to "Son of God." This was likely influenced by Chinese imperial titles and served to establish a claim to divinity or divine sanction for their rule.

questions.question: Which Gupta emperor was known as the 'Napoleon of India' due to his military conquests and strategic warfare?
questions.solution: Samudragupta was given the title 'Napoleon of India' by the historian V.A. Smith. This was due to his extensive military campaigns (Digvijaya) which brought a large part of the Indian subcontinent under Gupta suzerainty without him ever facing a major defeat.

questions.question: Which of the following Vedic words literally means search for cows and also refers to battle?
questions.solution: In the Early Vedic period, cattle (cows) were the most important form of wealth. The word Gaveshana literally means "search for cows," but because cattle raids were the primary cause of conflict, the term evolved to mean battle or war.

questions.question: Microliths, which are tiny stone tools usually stuck on to handles of bones or woods to make tools like saws and sickles. These were mostly found in which age?
questions.solution: Microliths are small, sharp stone tools (usually 1 to 5 cm long) characteristic of the Mesolithic age (Middle Stone Age). These were hafted onto wooden or bone handles to create composite tools like spears, arrows, and sickles.

questions.question: Which of the following Buddhist texts is often called the 'Gita of Buddhism' for its importance and teachings?
questions.solution: The Dhammapada is one of the most widely read and best-known Buddhist scriptures. Due to its concise collection of sayings and ethical teachings attributed to the Buddha, it is often called the 'Gita of Buddhism'.

questions.question: The Vardhan dynasty, was also known by which of the following names?
questions.solution: The Vardhan dynasty, which reached its zenith under Harshavardhana, was originally known as the Pushyabhuti dynasty. It was founded by Pushyabhuti in Thanesar (modern Haryana).

questions.question: The Acheulian cultural tradition is primarily identified with which types of advanced and symmetrical stone tools?
questions.solution: The Acheulian tradition belongs to the Lower Paleolithic period. It is characterized by more advanced, bifacial, and symmetrical stone tools, most notably the hand-axe and the cleaver, which represent a significant technological leap over the earlier Oldowan tools.

questions.question: The term gomat in the Rigveda refers to which of the following?
questions.solution: In the Rigveda, wealth was measured in terms of livestock. The term gomat (literally "possessing cows") was used to describe a wealthy person, reflecting the pastoral nature of the early Vedic economy.

questions.question: Which of the following officials toured Mauryan provinces every five years to inspect administration and submit reports on provincial governance?
questions.solution: In the Mauryan administrative system, the Pradeshikas were high-ranking provincial officers. According to Ashoka's Major Rock Edict III, they were mandated to tour their provinces every five years to perform an audit of the administration and spread the teachings of Dhamma.
